---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Larnix — learn AI/ML, from zero, in your browser"
toc: false
section-divs: false
---

::: {.lx-hero}
# Learn AI/ML — from zero, in your browser.

::: {.lx-lead}
A free, open-access school that takes a complete beginner — on a low-spec laptop,
at ₹0 — from "what is AI" to building and shipping real machine-learning systems.
Plain language, one idea per chapter, and code you actually run.
:::

[Start learning →](modules/00-orientation/ch01-train-your-first-model.qmd){.btn .btn-light .btn-lg}
[Read the roadmap](https://github.com/venkathub/larnix/blob/main/docs/ROADMAP.md){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-lg}
:::

## Why Larnix

::: {.lx-features}
::: {.lx-feature}
[🖥️]{.lx-ico}

### Runs in your browser
Beginner chapters execute Python with **zero install** via Pyodide. No account, no
download — press *Run* and watch a real model train.
:::

::: {.lx-feature}
[₹0]{.lx-ico}

### Free, the whole way
The core path costs nothing: in-browser for the early modules, free Colab/Kaggle
for the GPU ones, and a free local fallback wherever a lesson touches an API.
:::

::: {.lx-feature}
[🎓]{.lx-ico}

### Beginner → frontier
Seventeen modules, from "what is AI" through classical ML, deep learning,
transformers, LLMs, RAG/agents, MLOps, and a build-your-own-LLM capstone.
:::

::: {.lx-feature}
[✅]{.lx-ico}

### Every example works
Each chapter ships runnable code with auto-graded exercises and a quiz — and every
example is executed in CI, so nothing on the page is broken.
:::
:::

## Start here

::: {.lx-modcard}
[M0]{.lx-modnum}

::: {.lx-modcard-body}
### Orientation — *Train your first model, in your browser*
🟢 Beginner · runs in the browser · ~20 min. Load data, train a scikit-learn
classifier, read its accuracy — your first complete machine-learning loop.
:::
:::

[Begin Chapter 1 →](modules/00-orientation/ch01-train-your-first-model.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Status:** Phase P0 — the platform, proven end-to-end on one sample chapter. More
modules land as the curriculum is authored. See the
[roadmap](https://github.com/venkathub/larnix/blob/main/docs/ROADMAP.md) and
[About](about.qmd).
:::
